seoTitle: How to Close and Start a Sprint in Jira
metaDescription: Learn how to complete a Jira sprint, handle unfinished issues, and launch the next sprint with a goal and dates set correctly.
intro: Closing a sprint sloppily can scatter unfinished work and mess up your team's velocity history. This guide walks through completing an active sprint, deciding where leftover tickets go, and starting the next sprint cleanly.

steps.heading: Review your active sprint board
steps.body: Open Active Sprints from the left sidebar and check your board columns to see what's actually finished before closing anything.

steps.heading: Complete the sprint
steps.body: Click the Complete Sprint button in the top right to open the closure panel and begin wrapping up the iteration.

steps.heading: Handle incomplete issues
steps.body: Review the summary of unfinished work and choose whether to send it back to the backlog or roll it into a future sprint.

steps.heading: Confirm the sprint closure
steps.body: Click the final confirm button to close out the sprint, which also generates your team's sprint report automatically.

steps.heading: Start the next sprint
steps.body: Go to the backlog, locate your next planned sprint, set a goal and dates, and click Start Sprint to move into the new cycle.

faq.question: What happens to unfinished issues when I close a Jira sprint?
faq.answer: You'll be prompted to choose whether incomplete issues move back to the backlog or roll forward into a future sprint.

faq.question: Does closing a sprint affect my team's velocity history?
faq.answer: Sprint transitions are designed to keep historical velocity data intact as long as you close the sprint properly rather than deleting it.

faq.question: Where do I start a new sprint after closing the old one?
faq.answer: Go to the backlog tab, find your next pre-created sprint, set the goal and dates, and click Start Sprint.

faq.question: Can I set a sprint goal before starting it?
faq.answer: Yes, when starting a new sprint you can add a goal along with the workweek dates before confirming.
